title: toxicodendron sempervirens
intro: Toxicodendron sempervirens, also known as the poison sumac plant, is a species of woody shrub or small tree native to southeastern North America. The plant contains urushiol, an allergenic compound that causes severe skin irritation and allergic reactions, making it highly toxic. Despite its toxicity, Toxicodendron sempervirens has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis. The plant is often prepared into herbal remedies, such as teas, ointments, and salves, which are used to treat various health issues, although its use requires caution and proper handling due to its toxic properties.
